name: Federal Reserve
summary: Day 63 Thursday. FOMC HELD 3.5-3.75% WITH FOUR-WAY DISSENT — MOST SPLINTERED FOMC SINCE OCTOBER 1992. POWELL: 'IT HASN'T EVEN PEAKED YET' ON ENERGY INFLATION = EXPLICIT STRUCTURAL FRAMING (not transitory). 11-1 vote on rate (Stephen Miran lone Trump-nominee dovish dissent for 25bp cut); 4 dissents on statement collectively (Hammack/Cleveland, Kashkari/Minneapolis, Logan/Dallas voted with majority on rate but OPPOSED inclusion of easing bias — HAWKISH dissents). The 3:1 hawkish-vs-dovish dissent ratio is the operative signal — even Fed officials disagreeing with the statement language want it MORE hawkish. Powell confirmed final press conference as Chair; Warsh-transition 'very normal, standard.' POWELL'S STRUCTURAL FRAMING IS THE INVERSE OF YESTERDAY'S 'TRANSITORY' BINARY — yesterday's brief flagged 'transitory vs structural' as the single highest-information-density variable; Powell chose STRUCTURAL on the record. Cascade: DXY firmed +0.4% to 98.99 (breaks 98.7 ceiling); US 10Y +7bps to 4.42% (highest in 3M); CME FedWatch 0% Apr cut, 30% by Dec (down from 70% in Mar); 'Fed pivot is the next catalyst' narrative STRUCTURALLY REJECTED. For gold INFLATION-regime call: Day 10 of empirical confirmation; Powell's verbal validation (gold trapped under $4,600). For INR: no DXY-relief vector → ₹94.82 holds, ₹0.18 from ₹95. For Indian equities: removes DXY-relief vector flagged as potential catalyst. For US 10Y: pricing structural inflation acceptance; 4.42% close highest in 3M. Goldman gold regime call empirically vindicated Day 10. The gold $4,600 trigger downgraded yesterday from active to watching is correct AND should now be moved to RESOLVED-INACTIVE until regime shift signal (EM-Asia FX-stress contagion, Gulf bank stress, US-counterparty disclosure). For investor: 'Fed pivot' is structurally rejected; deferred minimum to Sep, more likely Dec. Mag 7 beat-and-raise capex (Meta + Alphabet + AWS + MSFT all raised AI capex; combined $630-650B 2026 commitment) PARTIALLY offset the hawkish FOMC read for tech valuations — net flat; SMH +1.7% recovered Apr 28 -3% selloff. SemiAnalysis Patel counter-frame: hyperscaler FCF compresses to ~zero by 2027 (capex consumes operating cash) — equity-multiple risk on hyperscalers heightened by zero-FCF dynamics. Q1 GDP advance estimate due Apr 30 (1.8-2.1% consensus). PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): FOMC announces decision Wed Apr 29 at 14:00 ET / 22:30 IST, Powell press conference 14:30 ET / 23:00 IST (Polymarket 99.9% probability of hold at 3.50-3.75% for 3rd consecutive session). POWELL'S LIKELY-FINAL FOMC AS CHAIR — Kevin Warsh designated successor when Powell's term ends May 15. Two big questions: (a) will Powell vacate his Board seat after his Chair term ends (signal of independence from Trump pressure), (b) HOW POWELL FRAMES HORMUZ-DRIVEN INFLATION — 'transitory' (bullish equities, allows future cuts) vs 'structural' (bearish equities, anchored hold). Polymarket prices ~30% chance of at least one rate cut by year-end vs ~70% holds. CONTEXT: March 2026 CPI 3.3% YoY (highest since May 2024) on oil shocks. Brent breached $115 intraday Wed (close $114.64 +3.04%) — 8th straight up session, highest since June 2022; Goldman raised late-2026 forecast to $90 from $80, institutionalizes 'higher for longer'; Goldman estimates Iran has curtailed up to 2.5M bpd. The energy-pass-through tail is THICKER, not thinner — 'transitory' framing structurally HARDER to sustain. INVESTOR-RELEVANCE: Powell's framing is the single most important downstream variable for: (a) US 10Y yield direction, (b) DXY direction, (c) USD/INR direction (transitory = INR bid; structural = INR pressured toward ₹95.00), (d) Indian equities (transitory = bid; structural = pressured), (e) gold (transitory = modest bid; structural = trapped under real-yield rise). Wed US 10Y 4.35% (+0.4% 1D); US 2Y 3.59%. June FOMC dovish-pivot probability NOW 25-30% (was 30-35% yesterday) on continued Brent escalation. NON-SEP meeting (no dot plot); forward-guidance language is sole catalyst. UAE OPEC exit Tue Apr 28 — UAE-Bessent dollar-swap-line is the Treasury-side financial-architecture leg of Trump-UAE-axis. Trump told aides Wed to PREPARE EXTENDED naval blockade (CONFIRMED Bloomberg/WaPo/NPR/CBS) — extends operational Hormuz disruption. The Bond market reaction is the cleanest read: 10Y at 4.35% +0.4% 1D is steepening reflex on oil-inflation; if FOMC text leans hawkish on energy, 10Y tests 4.40-4.45% within 24 hours. HIGHER-FOR-LONGER OIL REGIME RAISES ENERGY-PASS-THROUGH RISK INTO FORWARD GUIDANCE. CRITICAL CORRECTION: yesterday's $100.47 Brent print was a yfinance contract-roll glitch; live tape Mon $108.23 (+2.75%) / Tue $109.39 (+1.08%) — oil REVERSED UP, not down. Goldman raised late-2026 Brent forecast to $90 from $80 — institutionalizes 'higher for longer' as bank-house base case; Goldman estimates Iran has curtailed up to 2.5M bpd. The 'transitory geopolitical pricing' framing that yesterday's brief expected to survive April 30 is now structurally HARDER to sustain — Brent at $109 + Goldman base-case at $90 makes the energy-pass-through tail thicker, not thinner. Rate 3.5-3.75% unchanged; Polymarket 99.7% pause priced; NON-SEP meeting (no dot plot) — forward-guidance language is the sole catalyst. Tue US 10Y 4.34% (+0.6% — slight steepening on oil-inflation reflex); US 2Y 3.59% (anchored on Fed-on-hold). Q1 GDP advance estimate Wed April 29 (1.8-2.1% consensus) follows FOMC. Powell succession term expires May 15, 2026 (~2.5 weeks); Trump Kevin Warsh-line nominee likely hawkish-to-neutral continuity. June FOMC dovish-pivot scenario probability moves DOWN under higher-for-longer (was 40-45% on yesterday's Branch 3 framing, now 30-35%). Bond market reaction is the cleanest read: 10Y at 4.34% +0.6% 1D is the steepening reflex on oil-inflation; if the FOMC text leans hawkish on energy, 10Y tests 4.40-4.45% within 24 hours. Goldman gold regime call empirically vindicated Day 8 — bond market continues to interpret oil as transitory inflation event despite the higher-for-longer institutionalization. Rate 3.5-3.75% unchanged; Polymarket 99.7% pause priced; this is a NON-SEP meeting (no dot plot, no SEP) — forward-guidance language is the sole catalyst. Friday US 10Y 4.31% (-0.3%); US 2Y 3.59%. Powell succession term expires May 15, 2026 (~2.5 weeks). Q1 GDP advance estimate Wed April 29 (1.8-2.1% consensus). Branch 3 resolution (Brent gap -4.6% to $100.47 on Sunday Globex; crisis premium $35 → $30) reduces inflation pass-through pressure into the FOMC statement — supports 'transitory geopolitical pricing' framing surviving April 30. The forward-guidance language tape risk: a Fed shifting toward 'data-dependent / energy-price-sensitive' would still re-price S&P risk-off, but Branch 3 reduces this probability. Goldman gold regime call empirically vindicated Day 7 — bond market interpretation of oil as transitory inflation event holds. Post-FOMC: Powell succession May 15 trims maneuver room; Trump's Kevin Warsh-line nominee likely hawkish-to-neutral continuity. Rate-cut window: with Brent on Branch 3 path, June FOMC dovish-pivot scenario re-opens (was 35% pre-Branch-3; could rise to 40-45% on dovish FOMC language).

signals.content: FOMC voted 11-1 to hold rates at 3.50-3.75%. Projected one rate reduction in 2026 and another in 2027. But markets don't believe even one cut.
Market removed basically every rate cut for 2026, now pricing odds of a HIKE. Global bond yields at highest since May 2024. Pandemonium in bond markets.

Fed remains trapped -- 10Y at 4.44%, oil at $112.57, VIX at 31.05. Can't cut (inflation), can't hike (growth risk). ECB froze rate cuts March 19. RBI holding at 5.25%. All three major central banks on hold = global monetary policy paralysis. $200B Pentagon supplemental adds fiscal pressure. Section 122 tariffs adding 0.5-0.6% to price level (Yale Budget Lab). Vance: war will continue 'a little while longer' -- no near-term resolution to Fed's trap.

Stagflation trap deepening. Fed held at 3.5-3.75% (March 18). Market now pricing 52% probability of HIKE by year-end -- first time crossing 50% threshold. Powell: inflation 'not coming down as hoped.' ECB expected to HIKE 25bp April and June (Goldman, ABN AMRO) -- reversing easing path. Fed, ECB, and RBI ALL on hold simultaneously -- global monetary policy paralysis. Oil shock prevents rate cuts that would otherwise support growth. CONFIRMED.

Eurozone inflation surged to 2.5% (Eurostat flash estimate), energy component exploded +4.9% (from -3.1% in February) -- 8.0pp swing in one month. Most dramatic energy inflation print since 2022. Some analysts predict ECB rate HIKES at April and June meetings. Fed still trapped in stagflation: can't cut (oil above $100 = inflation), can't hike aggressively (growth risk). Pezeshkian peace signal briefly revived rate-cut conversation -- if oil drops below $85, Fed can resume cuts. But Hormuz decoupling means oil may stay elevated even with ceasefire -> Fed remains trapped. All three major central banks (Fed, ECB, RBI) frozen simultaneously. DXY dropped below 100 to 99.79 -- dollar weakness despite elevated yields suggests market pricing growth concerns over inflation. (Sources: Eurostat, CNBC, Euronews)

Fed trapped in stagflation -- ISM Prices Index at 78.3 (highest since June 2022, 19.3-point surge in 2 months) but employment contracting. 17 of 18 industries reported higher costs. Market pricing 52% probability of Fed HIKE by year-end for first time >50%. Bond market diverging from equity market -- yields fell -2bps on peace day (pricing recession) while equities rallied (pricing de-escalation). Rate differential with ECB (3.5-3.75% vs 2.0%) persists. When bond and equity markets disagree, bonds are usually right.

DXY fell to 99.54 (-0.42%), below the psychologically significant 100 level. Dollar weakness despite elevated yields is a growth-concern signal, not an inflation-relief signal. Provides mechanical support for gold ($4,758), oil, and other dollar-denominated commodities. The DXY-below-100 regime is the most parsimonious explanation for gold + equities rallying simultaneously.

POST-ADDRESS: DXY surged back above 100 to 100.22 (+0.6%) after Trump 'stone ages' address. Oil +8.3% -> inflation expectations spiked -> safe-haven dollar flow -> DXY above 100. The brief dip below 100 that mechanically supported gold is REVERSED. Gold crashed -3.1% to $4,636 as the oil->inflation->dollar->gold-down channel reactivated. US 10Y at 4.32% (+0.2%) -- barely moved, pricing neither inflation relief nor recession conviction. VIX surged +11.4% to 27.34 -- approaching 30 institutional threshold. (Sources: yfinance, TradingEconomics)

ISM Manufacturing Prices Index hit 78.3 -- highest since June 2022, a 19.3-point surge in two months. Employment stays in contraction -- textbook stagflation. Market pricing 52% probability of a Fed HIKE by year-end -- first time above 50%. US 10Y yield at 4.30% -- long end pricing growth deterioration. 2Y barely moved (3.61%) -- short end anchored on policy. The bond-equity divergence: bonds pricing damage already baked in, equities celebrating peace headlines. Fed trapped same as RBI and ECB -- can't cut (inflation), can't hike aggressively (growth). Oil at $109+ sustains the trap. (Sources: ISM, TradingEconomics, Bloomberg)

ISM Prices Index at 78.3 (highest since June 2022) foreshadows hot CPI print on April 10 -- the next binary event for Fed policy. Oil back at $109-112 after deadline extension keeps inflation elevated. Fed trapped in stagflation: can't cut with oil-driven inflation running through 17 of 18 industries, can't hike without crashing equities already down 4-5% monthly. Market-implied hike probability holding at ~52%. US gasoline at $4.13/gallon national average -- politically sensitive level. OPEC+ 206K bpd increase is symbolic (<2% of disrupted supply), confirming supply-side cannot rescue inflation. The April 10 CPI print will determine whether the 52% hike probability tips above or below the threshold -- a hot print with oil at $109+ makes a June hike a live possibility. Gold at $4,783 futures (rising despite DXY above 100) reflects market's verdict that the Fed is paralyzed. (Sources: ISM, Trading Economics, Epoch Times, Barchart)

JP Morgan forecasts March CPI headline jumping from 2.4% to 3.4% YoY on 11% energy component -- largest monthly jump since 2022 inflation spike. March NFP blowout: +178K vs +60K expected -- strong jobs CLOSED rate-cut door. ISM Prices Index at 78.3 (highest since June 2022). ISM Employment contracting while NFP strong = textbook late-cycle divergence. 52% probability of Fed HIKE by year-end. Cleveland Fed inflation nowcasting tracking hot. April 10 CPI print will dominate mid-week -- if above 3.4%, hike probability rises sharply. Stagflation comparisons to 1970s circulating. FOMC minutes also due this week. Fed in impossible position: hike into war-induced recession or let inflation run. (Sources: FinancialJuice, Cleveland Fed, yfinance)

March CPI release April 10 (T-3) -- JP Morgan forecasts headline jumping from 2.4% to 3.4% YoY on 11% energy component. Largest monthly jump since 2022 inflation spike. ISM Prices Index at 78.3 = highest since June 2022. With WTI now $115/Brent $111 and the DXY holding 100.08, the dollar-strength paradox is feeding back into Fed policy expectations: rate hike probability rises if headline > 2.6%. The gold cooling to $4,685 from $4,796 record reflects exactly this -- rising inflation expectations + rising rate expectations + DXY strength = compressed gold opportunity cost. Q1 earnings start April 10 (banks first); financials face credit concerns from war stress. The Fed has not commented publicly but the rate expectations curve has shifted hawkish.

EVENING UPDATE: Goldman raised US recession to 30% (3rd revision from 20->25->30). Moody's Zandi: 48.6% -- 'recession more than likely by H2 unless hostilities end now.' Trigger: Brent $125 Q2 average = recession (currently $111, $14 gap). EY-Parthenon (Daco): 40%. Oxford Economics: world GDP 1.4% if war extends 6 months. Dimon (JPM letter): war may be 'defining factor' in global economic order. ISM Prices 78.3 = stagflation confirmation. Market pricing 52%+ hike probability on hot CPI. March CPI April 10 -- JP Morgan forecasts 2.4->3.4% YoY. (Sources: Goldman Sachs, Moody's, EY-Parthenon, JPMorgan, Oxford Economics)

FED RECESSION-VIA-OIL PATHWAY MATERIALLY WEAKENED BY CEASEFIRE [REPORTED]. Pre-ceasefire: Goldman 30% / Moody's 48.6% recession probability driven primarily by Brent $115+ assumption. Post-ceasefire: Brent $94, Moody's Zandi 'Brent $125 Q2 average = recession' trigger now $30+ away from current spot. The recession-via-oil mechanism is materially weakened. US 10Y barely moved (4.34%, +0.2%) -- bond market doesn't yet price big Fed dovish shift. US 2Y -0.2% (slight dovish nudge). DXY -1.1% -- dollar relief rotation. The Fed's 'patient hold while oil-driven inflation passes through' framing is intact; hot CPI Friday is now less likely to force a hike. Watch Goldman/Moody's revised recession forecasts in next 24-48 hours.

Rate-cut probability nearly doubled post-ceasefire: from 25% to 43% for at least one cut in 2026 (CME FedWatch). Rate-hike odds halved from ~25% to 14%. FOMC March minutes (released April 8 evening) revealed hawkish tilt -- several members discussed possibility of rate hikes if oil stayed elevated -- but with WTI now ~$96, the inflation persistence argument weakened. Citigroup: if oil continues falling, sees potential for THREE cuts starting September. Oxford Economics: baseline TWO cuts to preempt labor market weakness. April 29 FOMC: 98% probability of no change. This is the ceasefire's most durable market legacy -- it shifted the monetary policy distribution function. Friday April 10 March CPI release (consensus 3.2-3.4% vs Feb 2.4%) will test durability. (Source: CNBC, Benzinga, CME FedWatch, Citigroup, Oxford Economics)

March CPI RESOLVED at 3.3% YoY -- 0.1pp BELOW 3.4% consensus [CONFIRMED, BLS official release]. Core CPI 2.6% YoY, +0.2% MoM -- both 0.1pp below forecast. Energy +10.9%, gasoline +21.2% (largest monthly since 1967). Textbook oil-supply-shock print -- core well-behaved, headline driven by single exogenous variable. Rate HIKE fears 'off the table' per FinancialContent [CONFIRMED]. CME FedWatch: June cut probability dropped from 55% to 35%, rate hike near zero, April 29 hold 98.4% [CONFIRMED, CME FedWatch]. For RBI: benign core means Malhotra's dovish June cut pathway survives -- Fed won't hike. Market SEPARATED headline from core -- treating 3.3% as transitory war-inflation. MOST DANGEROUS positioning: if Islamabad fails and oil returns to $110+, May CPI 3.5-4.0% collapses 'transitory' narrative.

US March CPI 3.3% YoY with +0.9% monthly -- biggest monthly increase since June 2022. Cleveland Fed warned rate hike possible. NY Fed flagged energy pass-through risk. Market pricing: 52% probability of Fed HIKE by year-end (first time >50%). PCE expected to hit 3.5% by April on lagged oil pass-through. Islamabad failure means oil stays elevated -> rate cut expectations that powered the ceasefire equity rally are dead. Bond-equity divergence: bonds validated.

Fed stagflation trap persists despite deal-hope market rally. S&P +1.0% to 6,886 erasing ALL war losses -- pricing 'blockade forces deal' narrative based on single unverified Trump claim that Iran 'reached out.' Bond market REFUSES TO COMMIT: 10Y barely moved to 4.30% (-2bps). Bond-equity divergence at its widest: equities pricing resolution, bonds pricing uncertainty. Silver/gold moves driven by rate expectations (silver +2.1% outperforming gold +1.1%), not safe-haven -- confirms monetary channel dominance. DXY at 98.32 (below 99) mechanically supports risk assets and precious metals. Hike probability still >50% year-end. The 'transitory' CPI narrative survives ONLY if oil stays below $100 -- blockade operational with 31 vessels/day vs 80-100 normal creates persistent supply disruption risk. (Sources: CME FedWatch, Bloomberg, CNBC, markets.md)

Stagflation trap deepening. March PPI +0.5% vs 1.1% expected — GENUINE inflation relief. Core PPI +0.1% MoM remarkably subdued. But annual PPI 4.0% (highest since Feb 2023) confirms cumulative damage. Gasoline accounted for nearly HALF of headline PPI (+15.7%) — war-driven oil IS the inflation transmission mechanism. FOMC March minutes: MORE officials willing to consider rate HIKES. Cleveland Fed Hammack: 'inflation above target for 5+ years,' hike possible if gas prices persist. Goolsbee: may need to raise rates if inflation rises with low unemployment. Market pricing 52% hike probability by year-end (first time >50%). PCE forecast raised to 2.7%. Bond-equity divergence WIDEST of war: S&P +1.2% erasing all war losses says 'deal imminent'; 10Y at 4.26% barely moved says 'not sure.' DXY at 98.16 (below 99). (Sources: BLS PPI data, FOMC minutes, CME FedWatch, yfinance)

EVENING UPDATE: PPI confirmation data — headline PPI +0.5% MoM, 4.0% YoY (3-year high). Gasoline +15.7% accounted for nearly half of MoM increase. Final demand energy +8.5%. Core PPI only +0.1% MoM, 3.8% YoY — ex-energy, price pressure NOT accelerating. The split: oil shock driving ALL the inflation. If oil normalizes, PPI drops sharply. Fed's dilemma is single-variable: they can't control oil with rates. Bond-equity divergence WIDEST of war: S&P at 6,967 (near ATH) says 'crisis over'; 10Y at 4.26% says 'we'll see.' DXY 98.24 below 99. When bonds and equities disagree, bonds usually right. (Sources: BLS, CNN, FRED, CNBC)

FRIDAY FULL-SESSION SETTLES — HORMUZ-OPEN BOND RALLY [CORRECTING earlier Good Friday misframing]. 10Y Treasury 4.25% (-1.5% 1D from 4.31%), 2Y 3.60% (-0.3%), VIX 17.48 (-2.6%). Iran FM Araghchi's 'Hormuz completely open' statement at 17:00 EST triggered the largest single-session oil crash of the crisis (Brent -7.6%, WTI -11.3%, heating oil -12.8%) and bond market partially repriced the inflation-expectation channel. Key mechanics: oil crash = near-term CPI impulse fades = Fed cut window potentially reopens. But 10Y only -6bps while 2Y -1bp ≈ flat — bond market pricing MODEST duration rally, NOT a material front-end re-cut trade. Translation: bond market thinks oil relief is conditional (Iran reciprocal-measures caveat), not confirmed. If Monday Islamabad delivers, expect 10Y to 4.15-4.20% and 2Y to 3.50-3.55% on cleaner cut-path repricing. If talks fail, 10Y snaps back to 4.30%+. Fed-Powell transition narrative intact — Trump's Kevin Warsh nomination signals hawkish-to-neutral policy continuity. Dated Brent physical $132 remains inconsistent with this bond rally — the physical oil hasn't repriced, and if Monday disappoints, bonds retrace immediately. (Sources: markets.md Friday settles, yfinance, FRED, Fed Chair transition reporting)

FED APRIL 28-29 FOMC 8 DAYS OUT — 99.3% NO CHANGE; STATEMENT FRAMING ON BRENT $95 IS LIVE RISK [REPORTED]. CME FedWatch 99.3% probability no rate change. Live issue: statement framing — "temporary energy shock" vs "inflation persistence" vs "reason to delay cuts deeper into H2." Brent $95 Monday tests whether "temporary" framing survives March CPI +0.9% m/m (core +0.2%). Front-month oil +50% vs. smaller longer-dated rise = market still pricing temporary-shock; Fed minutes align. Tape risk: Fed speaker (Waller, Williams, Daly) walking back June-cut Monday-Tuesday → yield spike across curve independent of crisis.

BOND MARKET COMPLACENCY THROUGH BRENT ROUNDTRIP — 10Y 4.29% RANGE-BOUND DESPITE PHASE 2 KINETIC RELOAD [CONFIRMED]. US 10Y 4.29% Wed (+1.0% 1D), 2Y 3.60% (flat). Brent rondtripped $90.38 → $101.97 → $92.48 → $99.81 across 4 sessions; 10Y has held within 4.25-4.35% range. JPM at 34% probability of zero 2026 cuts (up from 10% pre-war). Q1 GDP advance estimate due April 30 (6 trading days out) — likely independent driver of Fed pricing reset regardless of Iran. Powell term expires May 15, 2026 (~3 weeks); Trump nominee selection is independent market event. April 28-29 FOMC: 99.3% no change per CME FedWatch. The bond complacency through Wednesday's Phase 1 (extension) → Phase 2 (IRGC kinetic) suggests Treasury desk reading the crisis as inflation event (not financial-system event) AND temporary shock — if Brent sustains $97+ Thursday, expect 10Y retest 4.30%+ as inflation-exp rebuilds.

BRANCH 3 RESOLUTION → FOMC APRIL 28-29 DOVISH-LEANING HOLD MOST PROBABLE [REPORTED, ANALYTICAL]. Brent gapped -4.6% to $100.47 on Sunday Globex / Monday Asia open — Branch 3 (positioning) defeats Branch 1 (structural break) and Branch 2 (theatrical band). Crisis premium compressed from $35 → $30 = re-rating of TIME-EXTENSION risk, not LEVEL risk. Polymarket 99.7% pause for FOMC remains priced. NON-SEP meeting (no dot plot, no SEP) → forward-guidance language is the sole catalyst. Branch 3 architecture supports 'transitory geopolitical pricing' framing surviving April 30 statement. The forward-guidance tape risk that yesterday's brief flagged (Fed shift to 'data-dependent / energy-price-sensitive') is now LOWER PROBABILITY but not eliminated. Q1 GDP advance Wed April 29 (1.8-2.1% consensus): a print at 1.8% lower bound would compound dovish-language positioning; print above 2.1% would dampen it. June FOMC dovish-pivot scenario re-opens (was 35% pre-Branch-3; could rise to 40-45% on dovish April 30 language). Powell succession May 15 ahead — Powell's incentive to telegraph dovish path on the way out is non-trivial. Bond reaction Friday: 10Y 4.31% (-0.3%) range-bound; 2Y 3.59% — bond market not yet repricing dovish-shift, awaiting FOMC text. (Sources: Senior Market dossier Branch 3; Polymarket; FOMC calendar; markets data)
signals.verification: REPORTED — Branch 3 mechanism inferred; FOMC April 29 statement is the resolution event.

signals.content: BRANCH 3 THESIS FALSIFIED → FOMC FORWARD-GUIDANCE LANGUAGE FACES HIGHER ENERGY-PASS-THROUGH RISK [CONFIRMED OIL REVERSAL + REPORTED ANALYTICAL]. Yesterday's $100.47 Brent print was a yfinance contract-roll glitch; live tape Mon $108.23 / Tue $109.39 — oil REVERSED UP, not down. Goldman raised late-2026 Brent forecast to $90 from $80 — institutionalizes 'higher for longer' as bank-house base case (Goldman estimates Iran has curtailed up to 2.5M bpd). The 'transitory geopolitical pricing' framing that yesterday's brief expected to survive April 30 is now structurally HARDER to sustain. Polymarket 99.7% pause priced; NON-SEP meeting (no dot plot) — forward-guidance language is the sole catalyst. Tue US 10Y 4.34% (+0.6% — slight steepening on oil-inflation reflex); US 2Y 3.59% (anchored on Fed-on-hold). Q1 GDP advance Wed Apr 29 (1.8-2.1% consensus). June FOMC dovish-pivot probability moves DOWN under higher-for-longer (was 40-45% yesterday, now 30-35%). Bond market is the cleanest read: 10Y at 4.34% steepening on oil-inflation; if FOMC text leans hawkish on energy, 10Y tests 4.40-4.45% within 24 hours. Powell succession May 15 ahead. Goldman gold regime call empirically vindicated Day 8 — bond market continues to interpret oil as transitory inflation event despite higher-for-longer institutionalization. (Sources: TradingEconomics, CNBC, Goldman commentary via TheStreet, Polymarket, Q1 GDP advance calendar)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED Brent correction + REPORTED FOMC text-risk analytical.

signals.content: FOMC HELD 3.5-3.75% WITH FOUR-WAY DISSENT (MOST SPLINTERED SINCE OCT 1992); POWELL: 'IT HASN'T EVEN PEAKED YET' ON ENERGY INFLATION = STRUCTURAL FRAMING [CONFIRMED via 6+ wire sources]. (1) FOMC voted 11-1 to hold rates at 3.5-3.75%, but FOUR officials dissented in some form — the most splintered FOMC since October 1992 (CNBC 'Fed meeting recap: Powell to stay on board' Apr 29; Fox Business 'Fed holds rates steady'; NBC News 'Fed keeps interest rates steady as Iran war fuels inflation'; CNN 'Key takeaways from Powell's last meeting as Fed chair'; Kiplinger; US Bank). DISSENT STRUCTURE: Stephen Miran (Trump nominee) lone vote for 25bp CUT (dovish; Trump's position in the room); Hammack (Cleveland), Kashkari (Minneapolis), Logan (Dallas) voted with majority on rate but OPPOSED inclusion of easing bias in the statement (HAWKISH dissent — wanted statement framed MORE hawkishly, not less). The 3:1 hawkish-vs-dovish dissent ratio is the operative signal. Fed acknowledged 'uncertainty tied to the Middle East, rising energy prices and low job gains' and noted inflation is 'elevated, in part from the recent increase in global energy prices.' (2) POWELL KEY QUOTE: when asked about price surges from the war, 'it hasn't even peaked yet' — explicit framing of energy inflation as STRUCTURAL not transitory. This is the inverse of the binary the April 29 brief flagged: Powell did NOT validate the transitory framing, did NOT signal cuts, and the dissents lean hawkish (3) rather than dovish (1). Powell also confirmed this is his last press conference as chair and that Warsh-transition will be 'very normal, standard.' (3) MARKET CASCADE: DXY firmed +0.4% to 98.99 (breaks 98.7 ceiling) on hawkish-skewed FOMC dissent; US 10Y +7bps to 4.42% (highest in 3M, pricing structural inflation acceptance); equity prints HOLD (S&P -0.0%, NASDAQ +0.0%) — Mag 7 after-hours beat-and-raise saved the close from being negative. CME FedWatch: 0% Apr cut (NEW CASCADE: rate-cut window pushed beyond Sep, more likely Dec, from Mar 70%). (4) IMPLICATIONS: For gold-INFLATION-regime call (yesterday Day 9 of empirical confirmation; today Day 10 with Powell's verbal validation on the record); the graph downgrade of the gold $4,600 trigger from active to watching (yesterday's update-graph) was correct AND should now move to RESOLVED-INACTIVE until regime shift. For INR: no DXY-relief vector → ₹94.82 holds Day 3 of post-₹94.50 breach; ₹95 ₹0.18 away. For Indian equities: removes DXY-relief vector. For US 10Y: structural inflation acceptance priced. For Mag 7: earnings beat-and-raise (Meta + Alphabet + AWS + MSFT all raised AI capex; combined $630-650B) PARTIALLY offset hawkish FOMC read for tech valuations — net flat. (5) SEMIANALYSIS PATEL BEAR-FRAME: $180-190B Alphabet capex consumes essentially ALL operating cash → Google FCF goes to 'basically zero' by 2027 (24/7 Wall St., Yahoo Finance, Latent Space podcast). Hyperscaler 2026 capex on track for $650B+ with memory at 30% (vs 8% in 2023-24). Bullish on conviction; bearish on near-term valuation cushion. The bull AI-demand thesis confirmed AND the equity-multiple bear thesis on hyperscaler valuations heightened by zero-FCF dynamics. (Sources: CNBC FOMC recap, Fox Business, NBC News, CNN, Kiplinger, US Bank, CME FedWatch, 24/7 Wall St., Yahoo Finance, Latent Space podcast, SemiAnalysis newsletter)
